questions.statement:
<p><strong>Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.</strong></p>

<p>Sombre</p>

<div><strong>select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.</strong></div>

<div>The more <u>confounded</u> employers always create havoc in an industry.</div>

<p><strong>From the options given below, find the closest substitute for the underlined expression.</strong></p>

<p>It is not surprising for <u>a teenager who stays from school without good reason </u>to invent fantastic tales to escape punishment.</p>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom that can be substituted in the following sentence.</strong></p>

<p>The young boy was <u>born with a silver spoon in his mouth</u>.</p>
options: born with a spoon stuck in his mouth /
born with a spoon that was not of gold /
born to not so rich parents /

questions.statement:
<p>Select the most appropriate idiom to fill in the blank.</p>

<p><span style="">Once she has the credit card in her wallet, she purchases some of the most expensive&nbsp;</span><span style="">things _________ and then begins to worry about how to pay back.</span></p>
options: at the drop of a hat /
with a hat in her hand /

questions.statement:
<p data-sourcepos="1:1-1:67">Select the most appropriate pair of homonyms to fill in the blanks.</p>

<p data-sourcepos="3:1-7:17">The students placed ________ backpacks over ________ outside the classroom before sitting for the exam.</p>

<p>Each sentence below has one or two blanks. Fill in these blanks with most appropriate word/ phrase from the four options that are given following each sentence.</p>

<p>___________ sighting an approaching car, some drivers tend to speed up.</p>
options: When instead of slowing down at /

questions.statement: Select the sentence from the given options that conforms to Standard English Usage.
options: Anxiety disorders different form normal feelings of nervousness or anxiousness, and involves excessive fear or anxiety. /
Anxiety disorders differ from normal feelings of nervousness or anxiousness, and involve excessive fear or anxiety. /
Anxiety disorders differ with normal feelings of nervousness or anxiousness, and excess fear or anxiety involved. /
Anxiety disorders are different from normal feelings of nervousness or anxiousness, and involving excessive fear or anxiety. /

questions.statement:
<p><strong>Fill in the blank&nbsp;with suitable preposition from the given alternatives.</strong></p>

<p>My sister lives in the building ______&nbsp;the end of the crosscut road.</p>
